heroHeadline: US Sanctions Colombian RSF Mercenary Networks; Berlin Conference Pledges €1.3B for Sudan
heroSubtitle: On the third anniversary of the Sudan civil war, the US Treasury sanctioned three individuals and two firms linked to Colombian mercenary recruitment for the RSF — disrupting the 'Desert Wolves' drone operator network — while the Berlin Conference concluded with €1.3 billion in international pledges; the SAF government rejected both processes, and the humanitarian funding gap remains critical with only 16% of the $2.9B UN 2026 appeal disbursed despite conference commitments
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia, RT Africa, Abren, Pravda Sudan, Radio Tamazuj, Amnesty International, Africanews, Wikipedia (Sudan Civil War Timeline 2026), NBC News, Middle East Eye, Deutsche Welle
